title: OpenClaw should skip late listener events instead of crashing after a run ends

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62840.

task.summary: Repair the listener path so late WhatsApp events are skipped safely instead of crashing the gateway.
task.instructions: Investigate why a listener callback raises after the active run ends, repair the guard behavior so late events are skipped safely, and preserve the healthy in-run event path.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as late listener events stop crashing the runtime.

visible_context.issue_text: A WhatsApp message arriving after the previous run finishes can crash the gateway with `Agent listener invoked outside active run`.
error_logs: Unhandled promise rejection: Error: Agent listener invoked outside active run
gateway exited immediately after a late listener event
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture models the guard behavior behind the late-event crash.

code_fault.description: The listener path raises instead of skipping events that arrive after the active run ends.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.

notes: Code-only localized reproduction of openclaw/openclaw issue #62840.
